title: Shingles
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives.
supports: Shingles is a painful rash caused by the varicella-zoster virus.
Shingles can cause pain, burning, tingling, and blistering rash.
Shingles can be contagious to people who have never had chickenpox or vaccination until lesions crust.
Complications can involve the eye, nervous system, pneumonia, hearing problems, or severe disease in high-risk hosts.
